description_p1: BPC-157 is a synthetic pentadecapeptide with the sequence GEPPPGKPADDAGLV, corresponding to a fragment of a protection protein originally isolated from human gastric juice. It has the empirical formula C62H98N16O22 and a molecular weight of approximately 1419.5 Da. The peptide is proline-rich and classified as a cytoprotective reference compound in preclinical literature.
description_p2: BPC-157 has been used as a reference standard in in-vitro and ex-vivo studies of endothelial nitric oxide synthase (eNOS) signalling, angiogenic responses, and Src–Caveolin‑1–eNOS pathway modulation in vascular tissues. It is also employed in cell-based assays exploring cytoprotection and stress-response pathways in gastrointestinal and musculoskeletal models.
description_p3: Supplied as an HPLC-verified lyophilized powder at >98% purity for research use. Typically reconstituted in sterile water or buffered aqueous media for in‑vitro applications, and stored lyophilized at 2–8°C, with aliquots maintained at −20°C after reconstitution, protected from light and moisture. Reference standard for in-vitro research on cytoprotective and angiogenic signalling pathways. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
seo_description: Synthetic pentadecapeptide (GEPPPGKPADDAGLV) reference standard for in-vitro cytoprotective and angiogenic signalling research. >98% HPLC. RUO.
faq_1_q: What is the amino acid sequence and length of BPC-157?
faq_1_a: BPC-157 is a 15-amino-acid peptide with the sequence GEPPPGKPADDAGLV, derived from a larger gastric protection protein.
faq_2_q: Which in-vitro pathways is BPC-157 commonly used to study?
faq_2_a: It is frequently used in vascular and cell-culture models investigating eNOS activation, VEGF/VEGFR2-related angiogenic signalling, and cytoprotective stress-response pathways.
faq_3_q: Is the identity of BPC-157 supported by public chemical databases?
faq_3_a: Yes. BPC-157 and its salt forms are listed in PubChem (e.g., CID 78169625 and CID 9941957) with standardized structural and physicochemical data.
ref_1: PubChem CID 78169625. BPC 157 trifluoroacetate. National Center for Biotechnology Information, 2024.
ref_2: Wu C-H et al., 2020. Modulatory effects of BPC 157 on vasomotor tone and Src–Caveolin‑1–eNOS signalling in rat aorta. Scientific Reports.
ref_3: Sikiric P. et al., 2024. Stable gastric pentadecapeptide BPC 157 pleiotropic activity review. Inflammopharmacology.

description_p1: Thymosin beta-4 (TB-500) is a highly conserved 43-amino-acid peptide originally isolated from calf thymus and encoded in humans by the TMSB4X gene. It has the sequence Ac-SDKPDMAEIEKFDKSKLKKTETQEKNPLPSKETIEQEKQAGES and a molecular weight of approximately 4963 Da. Thymosin beta-4 is an actin-sequestering peptide and a prototypical member of the beta-thymosin family.
description_p2: In research, TB-500 is used as a reference peptide in in-vitro and in-vivo studies of actin polymerization, cell migration, and cytoskeletal dynamics, and in models of connective tissue matrix deposition. It has been studied in experimental wound-chamber systems and other preclinical models investigating extracellular-matrix gene expression and tissue remodelling pathways.
description_p3: The peptide is supplied as a lyophilized acetate salt with typical research-grade purity ≥98% (HPLC). Reconstitution in sterile water or buffered aqueous solutions is commonly used for in-vitro assays, with lyophilized material stored at −20°C and protected from light and moisture. Reference standard for in-vitro research on actin dynamics and thymosin-family signalling. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
storage: -20°C lyophilized; avoid repeated freeze–thaw; protect from light
seo_description: Thymosin beta-4 reference peptide for in-vitro studies of actin dynamics and connective tissue pathways. ≥98% HPLC. Lyophilized. RUO.
faq_1_q: What is the relationship between TB-500 and endogenous thymosin beta-4?
faq_1_a: TB-500 is the research-grade synthetic form of thymosin beta-4, corresponding to the full 43-amino-acid human peptide encoded by TMSB4X.
faq_2_q: Which cellular systems is thymosin beta-4 commonly studied in?
faq_2_a: It is widely used in cell-culture and tissue models examining actin polymerization, cell migration, and extracellular-matrix gene regulation.
faq_3_q: Does thymosin beta-4 have a registered CAS number and chemical identifiers?
faq_3_a: Yes. Thymosin beta-4 is registered under CAS 77591-33-4 with defined molecular formula C212H350N56O78S in chemical databases.
ref_1: Hannappel E. et al., 1981. Complete amino acid sequence of bovine thymosin beta 4: a thymic peptide. Proc Natl Acad Sci USA 78:1162–1166.
ref_2: ChemicalBook entry for Thymosin beta 4, CAS 77591-33-4. ChemicalBook, 2026.
ref_3: Thymosin beta-4 overview. ScienceDirect Topics, Elsevier.

description_p1: Semaglutide is a synthetic peptide analog of human glucagon-like peptide‑1 (GLP‑1) (7–37) in which Ala8 is replaced by α‑aminobutyric acid, Lys26 is acylated with a C18 diacid-containing spacer, and Lys34 is substituted with Arg. It has the empirical formula C187H291N45O59 and a molecular weight around 4113 Da in its peptide form. These structural modifications confer high affinity for serum albumin and increased resistance to DPP‑4-mediated degradation while retaining GLP‑1 receptor agonist pharmacology.
description_p2: Semaglutide is widely used as a reference GLP‑1 receptor agonist in in‑vitro studies of class B GPCR signalling, including cAMP accumulation, β‑arrestin recruitment, and receptor internalization in GLP‑1R‑expressing cell lines. It has also been employed in ex‑vivo systems examining pancreatic and cardiovascular GLP‑1R signalling cascades.
description_p3: Research-grade semaglutide is typically supplied as a lyophilized peptide with ≥98% HPLC purity. It is commonly reconstituted in sterile water or aqueous buffer for biochemical and cell-based assays, and stored lyophilized at −20°C with reconstituted aliquots kept frozen and protected from light. Reference standard for in-vitro research on GLP‑1 receptor signalling and incretin-pathway pharmacology. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
seo_description: Modified GLP-1 analog reference standard for in-vitro GLP-1 receptor signalling studies. Lyophilized peptide, ≥98% HPLC. RUO.
faq_1_q: What structural features distinguish semaglutide from native GLP‑1?
faq_1_a: It incorporates Aib at position 8, Arg at position 34, and a C18 diacid-containing acyl chain attached to Lys26, increasing albumin binding and proteolytic stability.
faq_2_q: In which assay formats is semaglutide commonly used as a reference compound?
faq_2_a: It is used in GLP‑1R cell lines for cAMP, β‑arrestin recruitment, receptor internalization, and binding assays, and in ex‑vivo tissue models of GLP‑1R signalling.
faq_3_q: Where can structural and identifier data for semaglutide be found?
faq_3_a: Detailed structural, formula, and identifier data are provided in PubChem semaglutide compound and substance records.
ref_1: IUPHAR/BPS Guide to Pharmacology ligand entry for semaglutide, GLP‑1 receptor agonist.
ref_2: PubChem CID 56843331. Semaglutide. National Center for Biotechnology Information.

description_p1: Tirzepatide is a synthetic linear polypeptide incretin mimetic that functions as a dual agonist at the glucose-dependent insulinotropic polypeptide (GIP) receptor and GLP‑1 receptor. It is a 39-amino-acid peptide incorporating a C20 fatty-diacidated lysine side chain to enhance albumin binding and extend circulation time. The peptide has an approximate molecular weight of 4813 Da and is registered in PubChem under CID 166567236.
description_p2: As a dual GIP/GLP‑1 receptor ligand, tirzepatide is used in in‑vitro assays probing class B GPCR pharmacology, including cAMP signalling, β‑arrestin recruitment, and biased agonism at GIPR and GLP‑1R in recombinant and native cell systems. It is also applied as a reference compound in mechanistic studies of incretin receptor co‑agonism and receptor trafficking.
description_p3: Research formulations are supplied as lyophilized peptide with typical purity ≥98–99% by HPLC. For in‑vitro work, tirzepatide is reconstituted in sterile water or aqueous buffer, with lyophilized vials stored at −20°C and protected from light; reconstituted aliquots are kept frozen to maintain integrity. Reference standard for in-vitro research on dual GIP and GLP‑1 receptor signalling. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
sequence: 39-residue GIP/GLP‑1 analog with C20 fatty-diacidated Lys side chain
seo_title: Tirzepatide Reference Standard | Dual Incretin Peptide
seo_description: Dual GIP/GLP‑1 receptor agonist peptide reference for in-vitro incretin signalling research. Lyophilized, high-purity. RUO.
faq_1_q: What receptors does tirzepatide target in in-vitro assays?
faq_1_a: Tirzepatide is a dual agonist at the GIP receptor and GLP‑1 receptor and is used to study signalling at both class B GPCRs.
faq_2_q: Why does tirzepatide include a fatty-acid side chain?
faq_2_a: The C20 fatty-diacidated lysine side chain increases albumin binding and prolongs circulation in vivo, and is retained in research-grade material to mirror clinical pharmacokinetics.
faq_3_q: Which public resources document tirzepatide's structure?
faq_3_a: Structural and identifier data are available in PubChem compound and substance records and in IUPHAR/BPS ligand entries.
ref_1: Frias J.P. et al., 2020. Tirzepatide, a dual GIP and GLP‑1 receptor agonist: preclinical pharmacology. Diabetes Obes Metab.
ref_2: PubChem CID 166567236. Tirzepatide. National Center for Biotechnology Information.

description_p1: Cagrilintide is a long-acting, acylated analog of the human amylin–calcitonin family peptide that acts as an agonist at amylin and calcitonin receptors. It is a 35-amino-acid peptide with multiple non‑canonical substitutions and intramolecular modifications, and has the empirical formula C194H312N54O59S2 with molecular weight approximately 4413 Da. The peptide is cyclized and lipidated to extend pharmacokinetic half‑life.
description_p2: Cagrilintide is used as a reference agonist in in‑vitro studies of calcitonin and amylin receptor pharmacology, including cAMP signalling, receptor internalization, and receptor complex stoichiometry in cell systems expressing CTR/RAMP combinations. It is also applied in preclinical research interrogating amylin-family signalling in metabolic and gastrointestinal pathways.
description_p3: Research-grade cagrilintide is generally supplied as lyophilized peptide with ≥98% purity by HPLC. It is reconstituted in sterile water or aqueous buffer for in‑vitro work, with lyophilized material stored at −20°C and reconstituted aliquots maintained frozen and protected from light. Reference standard for in-vitro research on amylin and calcitonin receptor signalling. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
sequence: Modified 35-residue amylin-family peptide with lipidated side chain
seo_description: Long-acting amylin receptor agonist analog for in-vitro calcitonin family receptor research. Lyophilized, ≥98% HPLC. RUO.
faq_1_q: What receptor systems are typically interrogated with cagrilintide?
faq_1_a: Cagrilintide is used in assays on calcitonin and amylin receptors, commonly in CTR/RAMP heterodimer systems analyzing cAMP and related signalling outputs.
faq_2_q: How is cagrilintide structurally related to native amylin?
faq_2_a: It is a modified amylin analog incorporating non‑canonical residues, intramolecular constraints, and acylation to enhance stability and receptor engagement.
faq_3_q: Where is cagrilintide structurally characterized?
faq_3_a: Detailed structural descriptors and identifiers are available in PubChem compound and patent entries.
ref_1: PubChem CID 171397054. Cagrilintide. National Center for Biotechnology Information.
ref_2: CN-114249808-A. Synthetic method of Cagrilintide. Chinese patent summary via PubChem.

description_p1: GHK‑Cu is a copper(II) complex of the endogenous tripeptide glycyl‑L‑histidyl‑L‑lysine, forming a 1:1 peptide–metal chelate. The peptide ligand (GHK) has the empirical formula C14H24N6O4, while the copper complex is reported with molecular weight around 400.9–744.3 g/mol depending on counterions and hydration state. GHK displays high affinity for Cu(II), coordinating via the histidine imidazole and terminal amine groups.
description_p2: GHK‑Cu is widely used as a reference copper tripeptide in in‑vitro and in‑vivo models examining extracellular-matrix gene expression, connective-tissue remodelling, and metalloproteinase–inhibitor balance. It has also been employed in transport and penetration studies across model stratum corneum membranes, and in cell systems exploring copper trafficking and redox-related signalling.
description_p3: Research-grade GHK‑Cu is supplied as a lyophilized powder with typical purity ≥98% by HPLC. It is readily soluble in water and aqueous buffers, forming blue solutions characteristic of Cu(II) complexes, and is stored at −20°C protected from light and moisture to limit oxidation. Reference standard for in-vitro research on copper-peptide signalling and extracellular-matrix regulation pathways. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
seo_description: Copper tripeptide (glycyl-histidyl-lysine Cu(II)) reference standard for in-vitro copper signalling and matrix-pathway research. Lyophilized. RUO.
faq_1_q: What is the ligand structure in GHK‑Cu?
faq_1_a: The ligand is the tripeptide glycyl‑L‑histidyl‑L‑lysine (GHK), which chelates Cu(II) primarily via the N‑terminal amine and histidine imidazole nitrogens.
faq_2_q: In what types of models is GHK‑Cu commonly studied?
faq_2_a: It is used in connective-tissue and skin models, wound-chamber systems, and cell cultures analyzing extracellular-matrix gene expression and copper transport.
faq_3_q: Does GHK‑Cu have defined chemical identifiers?
faq_3_a: Yes. GHK‑Cu and related copper tripeptide entries are listed in PubChem with CIDs 378611, 133697840, and 139035031, among others.
ref_1: Pickart L. et al., 1991. In vivo stimulation of connective tissue accumulation by the tripeptide–copper complex GHK‑Cu2+ in rat experimental wounds. J Surg Res 50:216–225.
ref_2: Mazurowska L., Mojski M., 2007. ESI‑MS study of GHK‑Cu(II) complex transport through model stratum corneum membrane. Talanta 72:650–654.
ref_3: Pickart L. et al., 2018. Regenerative and protective actions of the GHK‑Cu peptide in the light of the new gene data. Int J Mol Sci 19:1987.

description_p1: MOTS‑c (mitochondrial open reading frame of the 12S rRNA‑c) is a 16‑amino‑acid peptide encoded by a short open reading frame within the mitochondrial 12S rRNA gene. The sequence is MRWQEMGYIFYPRKLR, and the peptide is synthesized in the cytoplasm using the nuclear genetic code before localizing to mitochondria under basal conditions. MOTS‑c is classified as a mitochondrial-derived regulatory peptide with reported interactions with metabolic pathways in preclinical models.
description_p2: In research, MOTS‑c is used as a reference peptide in in‑vitro studies of cellular energy-sensing networks, including assays examining AMPK activation, folate cycle modulation, and downstream gene-expression programs under metabolic stress. It is also applied in cell and animal models exploring mitochondrial–nuclear communication and stress‑response signalling.
description_p3: Research-grade MOTS‑c is supplied as a lyophilized peptide at high purity (≥98% HPLC) for experimental use. It is typically reconstituted in sterile water or aqueous buffer immediately prior to use, with lyophilized vials stored at −20°C and reconstituted aliquots kept frozen and protected from repeated freeze–thaw. Reference standard for in-vitro research on mitochondrial-derived peptide signalling and AMPK-associated pathways. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
seo_description: 16-residue mitochondrial-derived peptide (MRWQEMGYIFYPRKLR) for in-vitro studies of AMPK and metabolic stress signalling. ≥98% HPLC. RUO.
faq_1_q: What is the genetic origin of MOTS‑c?
faq_1_a: MOTS‑c is encoded by a short open reading frame within the mitochondrial 12S rRNA gene but is translated in the cytoplasm using the standard genetic code.
faq_2_q: Which signalling pathways are commonly interrogated using MOTS‑c?
faq_2_a: It is frequently used in studies of AMPK signalling, folate-cycle modulation, and metabolic-stress responses in cultured cells and animal models.
faq_3_q: How is research-grade MOTS‑c typically supplied?
faq_3_a: Vendors provide MOTS‑c as a lyophilized, >98% pure peptide intended for reconstitution in aqueous media for in‑vitro applications.
ref_1: Kim K.H. et al., 2023. MOTS‑c: a promising mitochondrial-derived peptide for therapeutic applications. Front Endocrinol.
ref_2: Lin J. et al., 2023. MOTS‑c functionally prevents metabolic disorders. Front Endocrinol.

description_p1: Epithalon (Epitalon, AEDG) is a synthetic tetrapeptide with the sequence Ala‑Glu‑Asp‑Gly, originally designed based on the amino-acid composition of epithalamin, an extract from bovine pineal gland. It has the molecular formula C14H22N4O9 and a molecular weight of approximately 390.35 Da. Epithalon is classified as a pineal regulatory peptide in the short-peptide literature.
description_p2: Epithalon is employed as a reference compound in in‑vitro and animal studies investigating telomere biology, chromatin regulation, and pineal‑associated signalling pathways. It has been studied in cellular models for its effects on telomerase activity and gene-expression patterns associated with stress resistance and circadian regulation.
description_p3: Research-grade Epithalon is typically supplied as a lyophilized powder with ≥98% HPLC purity. It is readily soluble in water and aqueous buffers, and lyophilized material is stored at −20°C with reconstituted aliquots maintained frozen and protected from light. Reference standard for in-vitro research on pineal peptide signalling and telomere-associated pathways. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
seo_description: Synthetic tetrapeptide (Ala-Glu-Asp-Gly) reference standard for in-vitro research on pineal peptides and telomere-associated pathways. ≥98% HPLC. RUO.
faq_1_q: What is the structural basis for Epithalon?
faq_1_a: Epithalon reproduces the tetrapeptide Ala‑Glu‑Asp‑Gly identified from analysis of epithalamin, a pineal-gland polypeptide preparation.
faq_2_q: Which cellular pathways are most commonly studied with Epithalon?
faq_2_a: It is used in cell culture and animal models examining telomerase regulation, chromatin state, and gene networks associated with stress resistance and circadian signalling.
faq_3_q: Is Epithalon registered in public chemical databases?
faq_3_a: Yes. Epithalon is listed in PubChem and other databases with defined structure, molecular weight, and identifiers.
ref_1: Khavinson V.K. et al., 2025. Overview of Epitalon—highly bioactive pineal tetrapeptide with pleiotropic geroprotective effects. Biogerontology.
ref_2: PubChem substance SID 135141173. Epithalon. National Center for Biotechnology Information.

description_p1: Sermorelin is a synthetic peptide corresponding to the 1–29 N‑terminal fragment of human growth hormone-releasing hormone (GHRH), amidated at the C‑terminus. It comprises 29 amino acids with molecular formula approximately C149H246N44O42S and molecular weight about 3357–3418 Da depending on salt form. This fragment retains the full GHRH receptor agonist activity of the native hormone.
description_p2: Sermorelin is widely used as a reference GHRH analog in in‑vitro pituitary and heterologous expression systems to study class B GHRH receptor pharmacology. It is applied in assays of receptor binding, cAMP accumulation, and growth-hormone secretagogue signalling pathways in somatotroph cell models and transfected cell lines.
description_p3: Research-grade sermorelin is supplied predominantly as the acetate salt in lyophilized form with ≥98% HPLC purity. It is soluble in water and dilute aqueous buffers and is stored lyophilized at −20°C, with reconstituted solutions kept frozen and protected from light to maintain peptide integrity. Reference standard for in-vitro research on GHRH receptor signalling and growth hormone secretagogue pathways. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
seo_description: GHRH (1–29)-amide reference peptide for in-vitro GHRH receptor and growth hormone secretagogue signalling research. Lyophilized, high-purity. RUO.
faq_1_q: How does sermorelin relate structurally to native GHRH?
faq_1_a: It reproduces the first 29 amino acids of human GHRH with a C‑terminal amide, representing the shortest fully functional fragment for GHRH receptor activation.
faq_2_q: In what systems is sermorelin commonly used as a reference compound?
faq_2_a: It is used in pituitary cell cultures and recombinant GHRH receptor-expressing lines for binding, cAMP, and downstream signalling assays.
faq_3_q: What are the common forms and identifiers of sermorelin?
faq_3_a: Sermorelin and its acetate salt are registered with CAS numbers 86168‑78‑7 and 114466‑38‑5 and are catalogued in PubChem with detailed structural data.
ref_1: PubChem CID 16132413. Sermorelin. National Center for Biotechnology Information.
ref_2: Wikipedia. Sermorelin. GHRH(1–29)-amide overview.

description_p1: CJC-1295 with DAC is a synthetic analog of human growth hormone-releasing hormone (GHRH) (1–29) that incorporates a drug affinity complex (DAC) moiety to enable covalent binding to serum albumin. The peptide backbone is a modified GHRH(1–29) sequence (e.g., Tyr‑D‑Ala‑Asp‑Ala‑Ile‑Phe‑Thr‑Gln‑Ser‑Tyr‑Arg‑Lys‑Val‑Leu‑Ala‑Gln‑Leu‑Ser‑Ala‑Arg‑Lys‑Leu‑…) with empirical formula C165H269N47O46 reported for the DAC-conjugated form. The DAC group is typically attached via a lysine side chain to form a stable albumin-reactive entity.
description_p2: CJC-1295 DAC is used as a long-acting GHRH analog in in‑vitro and in‑vivo models examining class B GHRH receptor pharmacology and growth hormone secretagogue signalling. It serves as a reference compound in studies of receptor binding, cAMP generation, and pharmacokinetic extension via albumin bioconjugation.
description_p3: Research-grade CJC-1295 DAC is supplied as a lyophilized peptide at ≥98% purity by HPLC, typically as an acetate or trifluoroacetate salt. The material is reconstituted in sterile water or aqueous buffer immediately prior to use and stored lyophilized at −20°C, with reconstituted aliquots kept frozen and protected from light. Reference standard for in-vitro research on GHRH receptor signalling and albumin bioconjugation strategies. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
sequence: Modified GHRH(1–29) peptide with albumin-binding DAC moiety
seo_title: CJC-1295 DAC Reference Standard | GHRH Analog
seo_description: Long-acting GHRH(1–29) analog with DAC for in-vitro studies of growth hormone secretagogue receptor signalling. ≥98% HPLC. RUO.
faq_1_q: What is the structural relationship between CJC-1295 DAC and native GHRH?
faq_1_a: CJC-1295 DAC is based on the 1–29 N-terminal fragment of human GHRH with sequence modifications and an added drug affinity complex that enables albumin binding.
faq_2_q: Why is the DAC group incorporated into CJC-1295?
faq_2_a: The DAC moiety forms covalent or quasi-covalent bonds with circulating albumin, extending the peptide's residence time and enabling long-acting receptor engagement in preclinical models.
faq_3_q: How is CJC-1295 DAC typically formulated for research?
faq_3_a: Vendors supply CJC-1295 DAC as a lyophilized, high-purity peptide salt intended for reconstitution in aqueous media for biochemical and cell-based assays.
ref_1: Wikipedia. CJC-1295 – long-acting GHRH analog with DAC.
ref_2: Particle Peptides safety data sheet for CJC-1295 + DAC, listing CAS 863288-34-0 and peptide formula.

description_p1: Ipamorelin is a synthetic pentapeptide with the sequence Aib‑His‑D‑2‑Nal‑D‑Phe‑Lys‑NH2, where Aib is α‑aminoisobutyric acid and 2‑Nal is 3‑(2‑naphthyl)‑D‑alanine. It has the empirical formula C38H49N9O5 and a molecular weight of approximately 711.85 Da. Ipamorelin is a highly selective agonist of the ghrelin/growth hormone secretagogue receptor (GHSR‑1a) and belongs to the class of non‑endogenous growth hormone secretagogues.
description_p2: In research, ipamorelin is widely used as a reference GHSR agonist in in‑vitro assays and ex‑vivo pituitary or hypothalamic systems. It is applied in binding, calcium-flux, and cAMP assays, as well as in perfused organ and animal models studying growth hormone secretagogue receptor signalling.
description_p3: Research-grade ipamorelin is provided mainly as the acetate salt in lyophilized form with purity ≥98% by HPLC. It shows good stability when stored at −20°C protected from light and moisture, and is typically reconstituted in water, dilute acids, or mixed aqueous–organic solvents for experimental use. Reference standard for in-vitro research on ghrelin/growth hormone secretagogue receptor pathways. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
seo_description: Pentapeptide GHSR agonist (Aib-His-D-2-Nal-D-Phe-Lys-NH2) for in-vitro growth hormone secretagogue receptor research. ≥98% HPLC. RUO.
faq_1_q: What receptor does ipamorelin target in research models?
faq_1_a: Ipamorelin is a selective agonist at the ghrelin/growth hormone secretagogue receptor GHSR‑1a and is used to study this receptor's signalling in vitro.
faq_2_q: What is the amino acid composition of ipamorelin?
faq_2_a: It is a pentapeptide composed of Aib, His, D‑2‑Nal, D‑Phe, and Lys, with a C‑terminal amide.
faq_3_q: How is ipamorelin typically supplied for laboratory use?
faq_3_a: Vendors offer ipamorelin predominantly as a lyophilized acetate salt with ≥98% HPLC purity, intended for reconstitution immediately before experiments.
ref_1: ChemicalBook entry for Ipamorelin, CAS 170851-70-4, including formula and sequence.
ref_2: CymitQuimica technical description of Ipamorelin, listing formula and molecular weight.

description_p1: 5-Amino-1MQ is a synthetic small-molecule research probe belonging to the quaternary quinolinium class. It is commonly referenced by the cation name 5-amino-1-methylquinolinium (C10H11N2+) and is frequently supplied as an iodide salt (C10H11IN2) with CAS number 42464-96-0. The compound has been discussed in preclinical literature as a substrate-site–targeting inhibitor candidate for nicotinamide N‑methyltransferase (NNMT).
description_p2: In vitro, 5-Amino-1MQ is used as a reference NNMT-modulating small molecule in enzymatic assays, metabolite profiling workflows, and pathway studies assessing methylation-related biochemical readouts. It is applied in cell-free and cell-based systems interrogating NNMT activity and downstream metabolite changes in controlled experimental settings.
description_p3: Research-grade 5-Amino-1MQ iodide is typically supplied as a solid powder with ≥98% HPLC purity. It is soluble in polar solvents such as water and DMSO, and stability data and catalog guidance recommend storage at −20°C in a dry environment to preserve integrity. Reference standard for in-vitro research on NNMT-associated biochemical pathways. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
seo_description: Quinolinium NNMT research probe (5-Amino-1-methylquinolinium iodide) for in-vitro nicotinamide N-methyltransferase pathway studies. ≥98% HPLC. RUO.
faq_1_q: What is the preferred chemical form of 5-Amino-1MQ for research use?
faq_1_a: Many catalogs provide the iodide salt of 5-amino-1-methylquinolinium, which offers defined composition and good aqueous solubility.
faq_2_q: What enzyme system is commonly interrogated with 5-Amino-1MQ?
faq_2_a: 5-Amino-1MQ is used in in‑vitro assays of nicotinamide N‑methyltransferase (NNMT) to explore substrate-site modulation and downstream metabolite shifts.
faq_3_q: Are there public database entries for 5-Amino-1MQ?
faq_3_a: Yes. The cation and its salt forms appear in PubChem (e.g., CID 950107) and commercial chemical databases with structural and analytical data.
ref_1: BlkcellResearch technical monograph for 5-Amino-1MQ, summarizing NNMT probe usage and identifiers.
ref_2: ChemicalBook entry for 5-amino-1-methylquinolinium chloride/iodide, CAS 42464-96-0.

description_p1: NAD+ (nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide, oxidized form) is a ubiquitous pyridine nucleotide cofactor composed of an adenosine diphosphate ribose moiety linked via pyrophosphate to nicotinamide riboside. The oxidized species has empirical formula C21H26N7O14P2− (for the anionic form) and a molecular weight around 662.4 g/mol. NAD+ participates in redox reactions as an electron acceptor and is central to dehydrogenase-catalyzed metabolic transformations in biochemical systems.
description_p2: In research, NAD+ is used as a reference cofactor in enzyme-kinetic assays, mitochondrial function studies, and in‑vitro models of energy metabolism. It is routinely employed in spectrophotometric dehydrogenase assays, sirtuin activity measurements, and other in‑vitro systems that require defined pyridine nucleotide pools.
description_p3: Research-grade NAD+ is typically supplied as the disodium or free acid salt in lyophilized powder form with ≥98% purity. It is highly water-soluble and generally stored at −20°C in sealed containers protected from moisture and repeated freeze–thaw; reconstituted solutions are kept refrigerated or frozen depending on experimental timelines. Reference standard for in-vitro research on redox biochemistry and pyridine-nucleotide–dependent metabolic pathways. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
storage: -20°C; dry; protect from moisture and light
seo_description: Oxidized nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide cofactor for in-vitro redox enzyme and metabolic pathway studies. Lyophilized, ≥98% purity. RUO.
faq_1_q: What is the primary chemical role of NAD+ in research assays?
faq_1_a: NAD+ acts as an oxidizing cofactor, accepting hydride equivalents in dehydrogenase and related enzyme reactions during in‑vitro kinetic studies.
faq_2_q: In which assay formats is NAD+ most frequently used?
faq_2_a: It is a standard component in spectrophotometric dehydrogenase assays, mitochondrial respiration studies, and diverse enzyme systems requiring defined pyridine nucleotide concentrations.
faq_3_q: How is research-grade NAD+ typically formulated and stored?
faq_3_a: Vendors supply NAD+ as a lyophilized powder that is reconstituted with water immediately before use and stored at low temperature in the dark to limit degradation.

description_p1: Semax is a synthetic heptapeptide analogue of the adrenocorticotropic hormone fragment ACTH(4–10) with the sequence Met‑Glu‑His‑Phe‑Pro‑Gly‑Pro (MEHFPGP). It preserves the core ACTH(4–7) sequence and incorporates an additional Pro‑Gly‑Pro motif at the C‑terminus. The peptide has been characterized as a regulatory neuropeptide analog with high affinity for copper(II) ions in solution.
description_p2: Semax is used as a reference ACTH-derived peptide in in‑vitro and ex‑vivo models investigating neuropeptide signalling, neurotrophin expression, and metal-ion–associated oxidative processes. It has been studied in cell cultures for its interactions with copper(II) and for effects on viability under metal-induced stress conditions.
description_p3: Research-grade semax is supplied as a lyophilized acetate salt at ≥98% HPLC purity for experimental work. It is water-soluble and generally stored at −20°C protected from light, with reconstituted solutions maintained at low temperature and used promptly. Reference standard for in-vitro research on ACTH-fragment neuropeptides, metal–peptide interactions, and neurotrophin-related pathways. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
seo_title: Semax Reference Standard | ACTH Fragment Peptide
seo_description: Heptapeptide ACTH(4–10) analog (MEHFPGP) for in-vitro neuropeptide and metal–peptide signalling research. Lyophilized, ≥98% HPLC. RUO.
faq_1_q: What is the sequence origin of Semax?
faq_1_a: Semax is derived from the ACTH(4–10) fragment and extended with a C‑terminal Pro‑Gly‑Pro motif, yielding the heptapeptide MEHFPGP.
faq_2_q: Which experimental systems commonly employ Semax?
faq_2_a: It is used in neuronal and glial cell models, as well as biochemical systems, to study neuropeptide signalling and copper(II)-associated oxidative stress responses.
faq_3_q: Does Semax form complexes with metal ions?
faq_3_a: Yes. A detailed study reported high-affinity complexation of Semax with Cu(II) in solution, associated with protective effects in metal-induced toxicity models.
ref_1: Wikipedia. Semax – ACTH(4–10) analog with sequence MEHFPGP.
ref_2: Palacios C. et al., 2015. Semax, an ACTH 4–10 peptide analog with high affinity for copper(II) ion and protective ability against metal-induced cell toxicity. J Inorg Biochem.

description_p1: Selank is a synthetic heptapeptide with the sequence Thr‑Lys‑Pro‑Arg‑Pro‑Gly‑Pro (TKPRPGP), designed as an analog of the endogenous tetrapeptide tuftsin (Thr‑Lys‑Pro‑Arg). The C‑terminal Pro‑Gly‑Pro extension enhances peptide stability while preserving the tuftsin motif. Selank is classified as a regulatory neuropeptide analog in the Russian peptide literature.
description_p2: Selank is used as a reference peptide in in‑vitro and animal models assessing neuropeptide signalling, synaptic plasticity markers, and neurotrophin expression. It has been examined in systems measuring brain-derived neurotrophic factor (BDNF) expression and in receptor-binding or gene-expression assays relevant to stress-response pathways.
description_p3: Research-grade Selank is supplied as a lyophilized acetate salt with typical purity ≥98% by HPLC. It is soluble in water and aqueous buffers and is stored at −20°C protected from light and moisture; reconstituted solutions are kept refrigerated or frozen according to experimental schedules. Reference standard for in-vitro research on tuftsin-related neuropeptides and BDNF-associated signalling pathways. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
seo_title: Selank Reference Standard | Tuftsin Analog Peptide
seo_description: Heptapeptide tuftsin analog (TKPRPGP) for in-vitro neuropeptide and BDNF-pathway research. Lyophilized acetate salt, ≥98% HPLC. RUO.
faq_1_q: What structural motif does Selank contain?
faq_1_a: Selank contains the tuftsin tetrapeptide Thr‑Lys‑Pro‑Arg extended by Pro‑Gly‑Pro at the C‑terminus, improving stability while preserving the core motif.
faq_2_q: What research areas commonly employ Selank?
faq_2_a: It is used in neurobiology models studying neuropeptide signalling, BDNF expression, and gene-expression changes associated with stress-response pathways.
faq_3_q: How is Selank typically formulated?
faq_3_a: Vendors provide Selank as a lyophilized acetate salt of ≥98% purity, intended for reconstitution in water or buffer prior to in‑vitro or preclinical use.

description_p1: Kisspeptin-10 (KP‑10) is a decapeptide fragment of the human KISS1 gene product, corresponding to residues 45–54 (Metastin 45–54) with the sequence Tyr‑Asn‑Trp‑Asn‑Ser‑Phe‑Gly‑Leu‑Arg‑Phe‑NH2 (YNWNSFGLRF‑NH2). It has the empirical formula C63H83N17O14 and a molecular weight of approximately 1302.4 g/mol. Kisspeptin‑10 is a high-affinity ligand for the G protein–coupled receptor KISS1R (GPR54), which plays a key role in the hypothalamic–pituitary–gonadal axis.
description_p2: In vitro, kisspeptin‑10 is widely used as a reference peptide in receptor-binding, calcium-mobilization, and cAMP signalling assays in KISS1R‑expressing cell systems. It is also applied in pituitary and hypothalamic models exploring GnRH neuron activation and downstream gonadotropin-related pathways.
description_p3: Research-grade kisspeptin‑10 is supplied as a lyophilized acetate or trifluoroacetate salt with ≥98% HPLC purity. It is soluble in water and dilute acetic acid, and is stored at −20°C protected from light; once reconstituted, aliquots are typically frozen to maintain peptide stability. Reference standard for in-vitro research on KISS1R signalling and hypothalamic–pituitary–gonadal axis regulation. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
seo_description: Decapeptide KISS1R ligand (YNWNSFGLRF-NH2) for in-vitro GnRH neuron and hypothalamic–pituitary–gonadal signalling research. ≥98% HPLC. RUO.
faq_1_q: What is the sequence of kisspeptin‑10?
faq_1_a: The human KP‑10 sequence is Tyr‑Asn‑Trp‑Asn‑Ser‑Phe‑Gly‑Leu‑Arg‑Phe‑NH2 (YNWNSFGLRF‑NH2).
faq_2_q: Which receptor does kisspeptin‑10 activate in research systems?
faq_2_a: Kisspeptin‑10 is a high-affinity agonist of KISS1R (GPR54) and is commonly used to probe this receptor's signalling in vitro.
faq_3_q: How is kisspeptin‑10 typically used in endocrine research?
faq_3_a: It is employed in pituitary and hypothalamic models to study GnRH neuron activation and related hypothalamic–pituitary–gonadal axis signalling.
ref_1: PubChem CID 25240297. Kisspeptin-10. National Center for Biotechnology Information.
ref_2: Dhillo W.S. et al., 2011. Kisspeptin‑10 is a potent stimulator of LH and increases pulse frequency. J Clin Endocrinol Metab.

description_p1: Oxytocin is a cyclic nonapeptide hormone and neuropeptide with the sequence Cys‑Tyr‑Ile‑Gln‑Asn‑Cys‑Pro‑Leu‑Gly‑NH2 (CYIQNCPLG‑NH2), featuring a disulfide bridge between Cys1 and Cys6. It has the empirical formula C43H66N12O12S2 and is classified as a member of the vasopressin/oxytocin peptide family. Oxytocin acts via the oxytocin receptor (OXTR), a class A GPCR.
description_p2: In research, oxytocin is used as a reference ligand in receptor-binding and functional assays investigating OXTR-mediated G‑protein signalling, intracellular calcium flux, and β‑arrestin recruitment. It is also applied in myometrial strip preparations, neuronal cultures, and ex‑vivo tissue systems to characterize oxytocin receptor pharmacology and downstream signalling cascades.
description_p3: Research-grade oxytocin is supplied as a lyophilized peptide, often as an acetate salt, with ≥98% HPLC purity. It is readily soluble in water and dilute acids, and is stored at −20°C protected from light and moisture; reconstituted solutions are typically kept refrigerated or frozen and used within defined stability windows. Reference standard for in-vitro research on oxytocin receptor signalling and vasopressin/oxytocin-family neuropeptides. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
seo_description: Cyclic nonapeptide hormone (CYIQNCPLG-NH2) for in-vitro oxytocin receptor and vasopressin-family signalling research. Lyophilized, ≥98% HPLC. RUO.
faq_1_q: What is the primary receptor target of oxytocin?
faq_1_a: Oxytocin is an endogenous ligand for the oxytocin receptor (OXTR), a G protein–coupled receptor studied in many biochemical and tissue-based assays.
faq_2_q: What structural feature stabilizes oxytocin's conformation?
faq_2_a: A disulfide bridge between residues Cys1 and Cys6 cyclizes the peptide backbone, constraining its conformation.
faq_3_q: How is oxytocin typically supplied for experimental use?
faq_3_a: It is sold as a lyophilized peptide of high purity, commonly as the acetate salt, for reconstitution in aqueous media before in‑vitro or ex‑vivo experiments.

description_p1: LL‑37 is a 37‑amino‑acid cationic α‑helical peptide generated by proteolytic processing of the human cathelicidin precursor hCAP‑18. Its sequence is LLGDFFRKSKEKIGKEFKRIVQRIKDFLRNLVPRTES, and it carries a net positive charge under physiological conditions. LL‑37 is the only human cathelicidin identified to date and is classified as an antimicrobial host-defense peptide.
description_p2: In research, LL‑37 is widely used as a reference antimicrobial peptide in in‑vitro assays evaluating bactericidal activity, membrane permeabilization, and peptide–lipid interactions. It is also applied in studies of innate immune signalling, nucleic-acid complex formation, and cytokine induction in various cell types.
description_p3: Research-grade LL‑37 is typically supplied as a lyophilized trifluoroacetate or acetate salt at ≥95–98% HPLC purity. It is soluble in water and aqueous buffers, especially at slightly acidic pH, and is stored at −20°C protected from light and moisture, with reconstituted aliquots maintained frozen to preserve activity. Reference standard for in-vitro research on antimicrobial host-defense peptides, membrane interactions, and innate immune signalling pathways. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
seo_title: LL-37 Reference Standard | Human Cathelicidin Peptide
seo_description: Human cathelicidin peptide (37 aa) for in-vitro antimicrobial, membrane-interaction, and innate immune signalling research. Lyophilized. RUO.
faq_1_q: What is LL‑37's sequence origin?
faq_1_a: LL‑37 is derived from the C‑terminal region of the human cathelicidin precursor hCAP‑18 by proteolytic processing.
faq_2_q: Which in-vitro assays commonly employ LL‑37?
faq_2_a: It is used in bacterial killing assays, liposome leakage experiments, and cell-based studies dissecting innate immune signalling and nucleic-acid complexation.
faq_3_q: How is LL‑37 typically formulated for laboratory use?
faq_3_a: Vendors supply LL‑37 as a lyophilized peptide salt of high purity that is reconstituted in water or buffer immediately before antimicrobial and mechanistic assays.

ref_2: Nijnik A. et al., 2013. The human cathelicidin LL-37 as a multifunctional peptide in infection and inflammation. Expert Rev Anti Infect Ther.

description_p1: KPV is a tripeptide corresponding to residues 11–13 of α‑melanocyte-stimulating hormone (α‑MSH) with the sequence Lys‑Pro‑Val (H‑Lys‑Pro‑Val‑OH). It has the empirical formula C16H30N4O4 and a molecular weight of approximately 342.43 g/mol. KPV retains the C‑terminal motif of α‑MSH and is categorized as a melanocortin-related peptide fragment in the experimental peptide literature.
description_p2: In research, KPV is used as a reference peptide in in‑vitro models studying melanocortin receptor signalling and inflammatory pathway markers. It has been examined in cell and tissue systems for its effects on cytokine readouts, NF‑κB pathway components, and epithelial barrier-associated parameters in controlled experimental contexts.
description_p3: Research-grade KPV is supplied as a lyophilized peptide at ≥95–98% HPLC purity. It is highly hydrophilic and soluble in water and aqueous buffers, and is stored at −20°C protected from light and moisture; reconstituted solutions are typically aliquoted and frozen to maintain stability. Reference standard for in-vitro research on melanocortin-related peptides and inflammatory-signalling pathways. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
seo_description: α-MSH(11–13) fragment (Lys-Pro-Val) for in-vitro melanocortin receptor and inflammatory-signalling research. Lyophilized peptide, ≥95–98% HPLC. RUO.
faq_1_q: What is the structural origin of KPV?
faq_1_a: KPV corresponds to the C‑terminal three residues (11–13) of α‑MSH and represents a minimal melanocortin-related peptide fragment.
faq_2_q: Which research areas commonly employ KPV?
faq_2_a: It is used in studies of melanocortin receptor engagement and in in‑vitro models assessing inflammatory-signalling markers and epithelial responses.
faq_3_q: How is KPV typically supplied and stored?
faq_3_a: Vendors provide KPV as a synthetic, lyophilized tripeptide with high HPLC purity, stored at −20°C and reconstituted in aqueous media before experiments.

description_p1: Bacteriostatic Water is sterile, non-pyrogenic water preserved with 0.9% (w/v) benzyl alcohol as a bacteriostatic agent. It is supplied in multi-use vials for laboratory reconstitution of lyophilized research peptides and reference compounds. The benzyl alcohol preservative inhibits microbial growth in multi-use vials, allowing repeat access over defined storage periods in research protocols.
description_p2: Bacteriostatic Water is used as a reconstitution solvent in in-vitro and ex-vivo research workflows requiring sterile aqueous diluent with defined composition. It is not a therapeutic product and is not characterized for human or animal administration.
description_p3: Supplied as a sterile clear colorless solution in sealed multi-use vials. Store at room temperature (15-25°C) away from direct light. Once entered, discard within 28 days of first use per laboratory best practice for preserved sterile solutions. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
storage: 15-25°C; protect from light; discard 28 days after first entry

faq_1_a: Benzyl alcohol at 0.9% (w/v) acts as a bacteriostatic preservative, inhibiting microbial growth in multi-use vials during the permitted in-use period.
faq_2_q: How is Bacteriostatic Water typically used in research workflows?
faq_2_a: It is used as a sterile aqueous diluent for reconstitution of lyophilized research peptides prior to in-vitro assay work, per published reconstitution protocols for each compound.

faq_3_a: Standard laboratory practice for preserved sterile solutions is to discard multi-use vials within 28 days of first entry, stored at room temperature away from light.

description_p1: The BPC-157 + TB-500 blend is a multi-peptide research preparation combining the synthetic gastric pentadecapeptide BPC-157 (GEPPPGKPADDAGLV, ~1419.5 Da) with the 43-amino-acid thymic peptide Thymosin Beta-4 (TB-500, ~4963 Da). BPC-157 is classified as a cytoprotective peptide fragment derived from a protein isolated from human gastric juice, whereas Thymosin Beta-4 is an actin-sequestering beta-thymosin family peptide encoded by the TMSB4X gene.
description_p2: Commercial research vials typically contain equal mass fractions of BPC-157 and TB-500 (for example, 5 mg of each peptide in a 10 mg lyophilized blend), corresponding to a 1:1 mass ratio. When normalized for molecular weight, this mass ratio translates to an approximate molar ratio of ~3.5:1 for BPC-157:TB-500. The blend is used as a multi-component reference standard in in-vitro and ex-vivo models where cytoprotective and actin-associated peptide pathways are studied in parallel.
description_p3: The BPC-157 + TB-500 blend is supplied as a lyophilized powder of high HPLC purity, intended for reconstitution in aqueous media for analytical and cell-based assays. Lyophilized vials are stored at −20°C protected from light and moisture, with reconstituted aliquots maintained at low temperature to preserve peptide integrity. Reference standard for in-vitro research on cytoprotective and thymosin-family signalling pathways. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
storage: -20°C lyophilized; -20°C reconstituted; protect from light and moisture
molecular_weight: Blend of ~1419.5 Da (BPC-157) and ~4963 Da (Thymosin Beta-4)

faq_1_a: The blend combines BPC-157, a 15-residue gastric cytoprotective peptide fragment, with Thymosin Beta-4, a 43-residue thymic beta-thymosin peptide involved in actin binding.

faq_2_a: A common research configuration contains equal masses of BPC-157 and TB-500 (1:1 by weight), corresponding to an approximate molar ratio of ~3.5:1 (BPC-157:TB-500) when molecular weights are considered.
faq_3_q: How is this blend typically used in laboratory research?
faq_3_a: It is used as a multi-component reference standard in in-vitro and ex-vivo models that investigate cytoprotective signalling alongside thymosin-family, actin-associated pathways.

description_p1: The Glow Blend is a three-component research preparation containing the copper tripeptide complex GHK-Cu (glycyl-L-histidyl-L-lysine–Cu(II)), the gastric pentadecapeptide BPC-157 (GEPPPGKPADDAGLV), and the 43-residue thymic peptide Thymosin Beta-4 (TB-500). GHK-Cu is a high-affinity Cu(II) chelate of the endogenous tripeptide GHK, whereas BPC-157 and TB-500 represent cytoprotective and actin-binding peptide classes, respectively.
description_p2: Commercial Glow Blend vials are often formulated at a 5:1:1 mass ratio of GHK-Cu:BPC-157:TB-500; for example, 50 mg GHK-Cu, 10 mg BPC-157, and 10 mg TB-500 in a 70 mg vial. When molecular weights are taken into account (GHK-Cu ~400–744 Da, BPC-157 ~1419.5 Da, Thymosin Beta-4 ~4963 Da), this 5:1:1 mass ratio corresponds approximately to a molar ratio on the order of ~62:3.5:1 (GHK-Cu:BPC-157:TB-500). The blend is used as a multi-peptide reference standard in in-vitro models where copper-peptide signalling, cytoprotective pathways, and thymosin-family mechanisms are studied concurrently.
description_p3: The Glow Blend is supplied as a lyophilized, water-soluble powder with each component manufactured to high HPLC purity suitable for analytical and cell-based work. Lyophilized vials are stored at −20°C, protected from light and moisture, and reconstituted with aqueous media immediately prior to use; aliquots are typically maintained frozen for short-term use. Reference standard for in-vitro research on copper tripeptides, cytoprotective peptides, and thymosin-family signalling pathways. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.

faq_1_a: The Glow Blend contains GHK-Cu (copper tripeptide), BPC-157 (gastric cytoprotective peptide), and Thymosin Beta-4 (beta-thymosin actin-binding peptide).
faq_2_q: What is the typical formulation ratio and approximate molar ratio?
faq_2_a: A common configuration uses a 5:1:1 mass ratio of GHK-Cu:BPC-157:TB-500, which corresponds roughly to a ~62:3.5:1 molar ratio given their different molecular weights.

faq_3_a: It is used as a reference preparation in experiments that simultaneously interrogate copper-peptide signalling, cytoprotective pathways, and thymosin-family peptide biology.

description_p1: The CJC-1295 + Ipamorelin Blend is a two-component reference preparation containing CJC-1295 NO DAC (also termed Mod GRF(1–29)), a 29-residue GHRH analog, together with Ipamorelin, a synthetic pentapeptide growth hormone secretagogue receptor (GHSR-1a) agonist. CJC-1295 NO DAC has a reported molecular weight around 3367.9 Da, while Ipamorelin (Aib-His-D-2-Nal-D-Phe-Lys-NH2) has a molecular weight of approximately 711.8 Da.
description_p2: Typical research vials contain 10 mg total peptide comprising 5 mg CJC-1295 NO DAC and 5 mg Ipamorelin, corresponding to a 1:1 mass ratio. When normalized for molecular weight, this equates to an approximate molar ratio of ~1:4.8 (CJC-1295:Ipamorelin), reflecting the lower molecular weight and higher molar concentration of Ipamorelin at equal mass. The blend is used as a dual-peptide reference standard in in-vitro and ex-vivo models investigating GHRH receptor and GHSR-1a signalling in parallel.
description_p3: The CJC-1295 + Ipamorelin Blend is supplied as a lyophilized powder of ≥99% HPLC purity suitable for reconstitution in aqueous media. Lyophilized vials are stored at −20°C protected from light, with reconstituted aliquots frozen or refrigerated as appropriate for the experimental schedule. Reference standard for in-vitro research on growth hormone-releasing hormone and growth hormone secretagogue receptor pathways. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.

description_p1: Retatrutide (LY3437943) is a synthetic 39-amino-acid polypeptide based on a glucose-dependent insulinotropic polypeptide (GIP) backbone, incorporating non-coded residues (such as Aib at select positions) and a C20 fatty diacid side chain to enhance albumin binding. It has the molecular formula C221H342N46O68 and a reported molecular weight of approximately 4731.3 Da. Retatrutide is classified as a multi-receptor incretin-family peptide.
description_p2: In functional in-vitro assays, retatrutide acts as a potent full agonist at three class B G protein–coupled receptors: the glucagon receptor (GCGR), the GIP receptor (GIPR), and the GLP-1 receptor (GLP-1R). cAMP accumulation studies in human receptor-expressing cell lines report EC50 values of 5.79 nM at GCGR, 0.064 nM at GIPR, and 0.775 nM at GLP-1R, with corresponding nanomolar binding affinities in radioligand-displacement experiments.
description_p3: Research-grade retatrutide is supplied as a lyophilized acetate or related salt at high HPLC purity for biochemical and cell-based studies. It is typically reconstituted in aqueous buffer or DMSO for in-vitro work and stored lyophilized at −20°C protected from light, with reconstituted aliquots kept frozen to maintain stability. Reference standard for in-vitro research on triple agonism at GLP-1R, GIPR, and glucagon receptors and incretin-pathway signalling. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
sequence: 39-residue GIP-based analog with Aib substitutions and C20 fatty diacid conjugation

faq_1_q: What receptors does retatrutide activate in in-vitro assays?
faq_1_a: Retatrutide is a full agonist at the glucagon receptor (GCGR), GIP receptor (GIPR), and GLP-1 receptor (GLP-1R), with nanomolar potency in cAMP assays.
faq_2_q: How is retatrutide structurally engineered relative to native GIP?
faq_2_a: It is derived from a GIP backbone with non-coded residues such as Aib and a C20 fatty diacid side chain that confers albumin binding and extended pharmacokinetic properties in preclinical studies.
faq_3_q: In what experimental formats is retatrutide typically used?
faq_3_a: It is used as a reference ligand in receptor-binding, cAMP, and signalling-bias assays across GLP-1R, GIPR, and GCGR-expressing cell systems.

description_p1: PT-141 (Bremelanotide) is a synthetic cyclic heptapeptide analog of the endogenous melanocortin peptide α-melanocyte-stimulating hormone (α-MSH). It is structurally described as Ac-Nle-cyclo(Asp-His-D-Phe-Arg-Trp-Lys)-OH and has the empirical formula C50H68N14O10 with a molecular weight of approximately 1025.16 Da. The lactam bridge between the Asp and Lys side chains constrains the peptide into a cyclized conformation characteristic of melanocortin analogs.
description_p2: PT-141 is studied as a non-selective melanocortin receptor agonist with prominent activity at MC3R and MC4R, both G protein–coupled receptors expressed in central and peripheral tissues. In vitro, it is used as a reference ligand in receptor-binding assays, cAMP accumulation studies, and signalling-bias experiments across melanocortin receptor subtypes.
description_p3: Research-grade PT-141 is commonly supplied as a lyophilized acetate or free-base peptide of ≥97–98% HPLC purity. It is soluble in water and dilute acids and is stored at −20°C protected from light and moisture, with reconstituted solutions maintained at low temperature. Reference standard for in-vitro research on melanocortin receptor signalling, especially MC3R and MC4R pharmacology. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
seo_description: Cyclic α-MSH analog for in-vitro melanocortin receptor (MC3R/MC4R) signalling research. Lyophilized peptide, ≥98% HPLC. RUO.
faq_1_q: What class of peptide is PT-141?
faq_1_a: PT-141 is a cyclic heptapeptide analog of α-MSH and is categorized as a melanocortin receptor agonist.
faq_2_q: Which melanocortin receptor subtypes are most commonly studied with PT-141?
faq_2_a: It is frequently used to probe MC3R and MC4R pharmacology in cell-based receptor-binding and cAMP signalling assays.
faq_3_q: How is PT-141 typically supplied for research use?
faq_3_a: Vendors offer PT-141 as a lyophilized peptide, often as the acetate salt, with high HPLC purity for reconstitution in aqueous media.

description_p1: Tesamorelin is a synthetic 44-amino-acid analog of human growth hormone–releasing hormone (GHRH) with an N-terminal trans-3-hexenoyl modification and a Tyr1 substitution, formally designated N-(trans-3-hexenoyl)-[Tyr1]hGHRH(1–44)NH2. It closely mirrors endogenous GHRH sequence while incorporating modifications that enhance peptide stability and pharmacokinetic properties. Tesamorelin has the molecular formula C221H366N72O67S and a molecular weight around 5135.9 Da.
description_p2: Tesamorelin is a selective agonist of the GHRH receptor (GHRHR), a class B G protein–coupled receptor expressed on pituitary somatotrophs. In vitro, it is used as a reference GHRH analog in binding, cAMP accumulation, and downstream signalling assays in GHRHR-expressing cell lines and pituitary cell models, enabling detailed characterization of GHRH receptor pharmacology.
description_p3: Research-grade tesamorelin is supplied as a lyophilized acetate salt with high HPLC purity, intended for reconstitution in aqueous buffer or sterile water immediately before use. Lyophilized vials are stored at −20°C protected from light and moisture; reconstituted solutions are kept refrigerated or frozen as appropriate for experimental timelines. Reference standard for in-vitro research on GHRH receptor signalling and growth hormone axis regulation. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
seo_title: Tesamorelin Reference Standard | GHRH Analog Peptide
seo_description: 44-amino-acid GHRH analog (N-(trans-3-hexenoyl)-[Tyr1]hGHRH(1–44)NH2) for in-vitro GHRH receptor signalling research. Lyophilized. RUO.
faq_1_q: How does tesamorelin differ structurally from native GHRH?
faq_1_a: Tesamorelin preserves the 44-residue GHRH backbone but adds an N-terminal trans-3-hexenoyl group and a Tyr1 substitution to improve stability while retaining GHRH receptor agonism.
faq_2_q: Which receptor is targeted in tesamorelin signalling studies?
faq_2_a: Tesamorelin is a selective agonist at the GHRH receptor (GHRHR) and is used in in-vitro systems to characterize class B GHRH receptor pharmacology.
faq_3_q: In what forms is tesamorelin typically supplied for research?
faq_3_a: It is commonly provided as tesamorelin acetate, a lyophilized powder of high HPLC purity for reconstitution in aqueous media.
